label: Model-predictive control

node.label: Model-predictive control
node.definition: Model-predictive control repeatedly optimizes a finite-horizon action sequence under a dynamics model, executes the first action, and replans from new observations.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Model-predictive control is to connect Dynamical systems & control theory, Optimization theory to downstream capabilities including Robotics & embodied AI.
examples: combined with Dynamical systems & control theory
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Dynamical systems & control theory, Optimization theory.

dependencies.rationale: Model-predictive control uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Model-predictive control uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Model-predictive control uses Latent world models & predictive dynamics as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
